claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 3 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 8 years old and does not live with you.
  - Charlie is 10 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 18 weeks but claimant regularly spends on child
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions.

Let's start with the basics:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have three children - Alex who's 3, Blake who's 8, and Charlie who's 10. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! You have three children: Alex (3), Blake (8), and Charlie (10).

Now let me ask you some questions to assess your eligibility:

**Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Now, let me ask:

**Do all three children (Alex, Blake, and Charlie) live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, not all of them. Alex and Charlie live with me at home, but Blake doesn't live with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. So Alex and Charlie live with you, but Blake doesn't.

**Can you tell me about Blake's situation - where does Blake live, and do you make any financial contributions towards Blake's upkeep (such as for food, clothes, pocket money, or accommodation)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Blake lives with their other parent. I'm not currently able to contribute financially towards Blake's upkeep at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. 

Just to confirm a few more details:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for any of these three children (Alex, Blake, or Charlie)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for any of them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. Let me gather just a bit more information to complete the assessment:

**Are Alex and Charlie both under 16 years old and not in any special circumstances such as being in hospital, care, or residential accommodation for extended periods?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, actually, Blake has been in hospital for a while now - about 4 months or so. But I still regularly buy things for them like clothes, toys, and their favorite snacks when I visit.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for sharing that important information. So Blake has been in hospital for about 4 months, and you regularly spend money on them during visits (clothes, toys, snacks).

Let me clarify a couple more things:

**First, regarding Alex and Charlie who live with you - are they both in good health and living at home with you full-time (not in hospital or care)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right. Alex and Charlie are both at home with me full-time. They're both doing well.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. Now, one final question to complete my assessment:

**Is Blake in a UK hospital, and approximately how much would you estimate you spend on Blake regularly (weekly or monthly) when you visit?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Blake is in a UK hospital. As for what I spend, I'm not really keeping track of exact amounts - I just buy them things they need or want when I visit, you know, whatever they need at the time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Good News! You Are Eligible for Child Benefit for All Three Children

Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex, Blake, and Charlie**. Here's the breakdown for each child:

### **Alex (age 3)** ✓ Eligible
Alex lives with you in the UK and is under 16. You meet all the basic eligibility requirements as you're responsible for Alex and they live with you.

### **Blake (age 8)** ✓ Eligible  
Even though Blake has been in hospital for about 4 months (which is longer than the usual 12-week threshold), there's an important exception that applies to you: because Blake is in a UK hospital and you regularly spend money on them (buying clothes, toys, and snacks during your visits), you can continue to receive Child Benefit for Blake.

### **Charlie (age 10)** ✓ Eligible
Charlie lives with you in the UK and is under 16. You meet all the basic eligibility requirements as you're responsible for Charlie and they live with you.

Since no one else is claiming Child Benefit for any of your children, you're free to make a claim for all three of them. I hope this helps, and I wish Blake a speedy recovery!

reasonings: Alex is 3 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them. The claimant meets the basic eligibility requirements as they are responsible for Alex and live with them. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 8 years old (under 16) and has been in a UK hospital for approximately 4 months (which exceeds the 12-week threshold). However, there is an exception to the rule that stops Child Benefit after 12 weeks in hospital: if the child is in a UK hospital and the claimant regularly spends money on them, Child Benefit can continue. The claimant confirmed they regularly buy Blake clothes, toys, and snacks during visits. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake. Therefore, the claimant qualifies for the exception and is eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.

Charlie is 10 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them. The claimant meets the basic eligibility requirements as they are responsible for Charlie and live with them. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Charlie.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with three children: Alex (3), Blake (8), and Charlie (10). Alex and Charlie live at home with the claimant full-time, meeting the standard eligibility criteria. Blake does not live with the claimant (lives with their other parent) and has been in a UK hospital for about 4 months. While Blake has exceeded the 12-week hospital threshold, the claimant regularly spends money on Blake during visits (buying clothes, toys, and snacks), which qualifies them for the exception that allows Child Benefit to continue. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for any of the children. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for all three children.
